items.title: 2024 Research Forecast Report: Global Healthcare Cognitive Computing Market Projected to Reach $150.25 Billion By 2035 from $10.81 Billion in 2024
items.author: Researchandmarkets.Com
items.description: The global healthcare cognitive computing market is expected to grow from $10.81 billion in 2024 to $150.25 billion by 2035, driven by the adoption of cloud services, increase in chronic diseases, and the need for advanced patient data analysis.

items.title: If I Could Buy and Hold Only a Single Stock, This Would Be It
items.author: Stefon Walters
items.description: The article discusses why Microsoft is a stock that the author would buy and hold for the long term, citing the company's diversified business model, strong customer base, and ability to weather economic downturns.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Microsoft's diversified business model, strong customer base, and resilience during economic downturns, making it a stock the author would buy and hold for the long term.

items.title: Why Credo Technology Rallied Over 37% in December
items.author: Billy Duberstein
items.description: Credo Technology Group, a provider of data center connectivity equipment, saw its stock surge 37.3% in December after reporting strong earnings and guidance. The company's unique technologies, including power-efficient active electrical cables, are gaining traction with hyperscale data centers.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights Credo's strong earnings and guidance, indicating that its unique data center connectivity technologies are gaining traction with hyperscale customers. This suggests the company is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for AI-driven data center infrastructure.

The article mentions that Credo partners closely with Microsoft on its data center communications technology, but does not provide any additional details on the nature of this partnership or its impact on Credo's business.

items.title: What's Wrong With UiPath's Stock?
items.author: David Jagielski
items.description: UiPath, a company focused on automating tasks, has been underperforming despite the growing demand for AI-related solutions. The company's growth rate has been slowing, and it lacks profitability, making it difficult to compete against larger tech giants like Microsoft and Salesforce, which are also investing heavily in AI.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: UiPath's stock has underperformed, with a 49% drop last year and a 62% decline over the past three years. The company's growth rate has been slowing, and it is struggling to turn a profit, making it difficult to compete against larger tech companies with more resources.
Microsoft's Copilot offers automation features that may compete with UiPath's solutions, potentially reducing the demand for UiPath's products.
Salesforce is also investing heavily in AI agents, which could overlap with UiPath's offerings and make it harder for UiPath to differentiate itself.
Gartner has raised concerns that up to 30% of generative AI projects may be abandoned this year, which could make it more difficult for UiPath to attract new customers.

items.title: 2025 Is Here. 4 Things Every Investor Should Do to Be Ready for Whatever Happens in the Stock Market This Year.
items.author: The Motley Fool
items.description: The article provides four key tips for investors to navigate the stock market in 2025, including diversifying their portfolio, keeping cash on hand, investing in AI-related companies, and maintaining patience and a long-term perspective.
